content:
First rewrite the condition and the target in terms of a single linear relation.  We have

1)  \(p(5)+p(25)=1906\).  
   Since 
   \[
     p(5)=125a+25b+5c+d,\quad
     p(25)=15625a+625b+25c+d,
   \]
   their sum is
   \[
     15750a+650b+30c+2d=1906
     \;\Longrightarrow\;
     7875a+325b+15c+d=953.
   \]

2)  We want to minimize \(\bigl|p(15)\bigr|\).  But
   \[
     p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+d.
   \]
   Eliminate \(c+d\) by using the first equation:
   \[
     d=953-7875a-325b-15c,
   \]
   so
   \[
     p(15)
     =3375a+225b+15c+\bigl(953-7875a-325b-15c\bigr)
     =953-4500a-100b.
   \]
   Let \(N=45a+b\).  Then
   \[
     p(15)=953-100N,
   \]
   so \(p(15)\) runs through all integers congruent to \(953\pmod{100}\), i.e.\ 
   \(\dots,-147,-47,53,153,\dots\).  The smallest absolute value among these is 
   \(\bigl|-47\bigr|=47\).

One choice realizing \(|p(15)|=47\) is for example
\[
   a=0,\quad b=10,\quad c\ \text{any integer},\quad d=953-3250-15c=-2297-15c,
\]
which gives \(p(15)=-47\).  Hence the minimum possible value of \(\bigl|p(15)\bigr|\) is 

\(\boxed{47}.\)
